title: Doellingeria umbellata
intro: Doellingeria umbellata, also known as the tall flat-topped white aster, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in eastern North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Doellingeria umbellata include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Doellingeria umbellata is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and can also be used as a tea or infusate to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Doellingeria umbellata is classified in the following way: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae, the subfamily Asteroideae, the tribe Astereae, and the genus Doellingeria. The genus Doellingeria is a group of flowering plants. The genus Doellingeria is often placed in the subfamily Cichorioideae or in the subfamily Asteroideae. Doellingeria umbellata is also known as the tall wood aster. This classification may be subject to change based on further research and revisions in the field of taxonomy.
